openemr_claim: One OpenEMR install serves multiple facilities from a single shared patient database, so a patient carries one identity across facilities in that install; no enterprise MPI or cross-instance/probabilistic patient matching is documented.
quote: Supports multiple facilities

openemr_claim: Documents patient merge via Admin->Patients->Manage Duplicates (Merge and Discard), but the merge is explicitly non-recoverable; no unmerge/reversal is documented.
quote: Note this is a non-recoverable operation. For production machines it is recommended that you backup your system (database and documents) before doing any patient data merging.

openemr_claim: Single shared database makes a demographic correction enterprise-wide by construction; prior-value/update history is captured by the audit log (patient record updated events with user and time), not a field-level demographic version history.
quote: Patient record created/viewed/updated/deleted

openemr_claim: Documents HL7 support generally and an HL7 v2.5.1 ORU results interface (commonly via Mirth Connect); native inbound ADT (A01/A02/A03/A08 and cancels) ingestion/routing is not documented as a built-in feature.
quote: OpenEMR supports HL7 and the ANSI X12 EDI billing standards.

openemr_claim: Ambulatory-focused; no documented inpatient transfer workflow that preserves an active encounter's medication list/allergies/orders/results across sites. The nearest documented mechanism is a CCDA transition-of-care summary via the Carecoordination module.
quote: When you receive a CCDA file you may create a new patient with it or merge the information in it with an existing patient's record.

openemr_claim: Documents a calendar for creating appointments with a per-appointment status (each status has a unique symbol; the status list includes no-show and cancel) and multi-facility/provider scheduling; equipment/room as separately bookable resources is not clearly documented.
quote: Choose the correct appointment status from the drop-down list. Notice each status has a unique symbol associated with it.

openemr_claim: Documents a procedure order/results interface using HL7 v2.5.1 ORU that differentiates preliminary/final/corrected results and returns results to the chart; deliberate misroute detection is not documented.
quote: receive and store general laboratory results (includes ability to differentiate preliminary results and final results and the ability to process a corrected result)

The audit log attributes each action to a user (actor) with patient ID, status, and description, and facility/organization is modeled via multi-facility config; a universal owning-organization/source-system stamp on every clinical and financial record is not documented as such.
quote: User Name - Name of the user who performs the task ... Patient ID - Patient Identifier

openemr_claim: ERA/835 re-upload is explicitly deduplicated (upload prevented if already loaded); general interface-level idempotency for replayed ADT/order/result/charge payloads is not documented.
quote: If the ERA file has already been uploaded it will be noted and the upload prevented!

Documents multi-facility support with facility-scoped scheduling restrictions (non-authorized users restricted to their configured Schedule Facilities); full per-facility isolation of formularies, charge rules, and templates is not documented.
quote: Supports multiple facilities

Documents a FHIR R4 (4.0.1) API exposing 30+ resources with SMART on FHIR v2.2.0 and bulk export (Group + Bulk Export operations); the FHIR README lists USCDI v1. ONC (g)(10) Standardized API is certified per CHPL.
quote: FHIR | R4 (4.0.1) | Full Support

openemr_claim: Documents unique per-user identity/authentication and supports two-factor authentication (TOTP/U2F); ONC (d)(1) and (d)(13) MFA are certified per CHPL.
quote: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity and establish controls that permit only authorized users to access electronic health information.

openemr_claim: Documents a role-based access control (php-GACL) system configured at Administration->ACL that gates read/create/update/delete/export across categories (admin, accounting, patients, encounters, sensitivities); ONC (d)(1) certified per CHPL.
quote: Access controls are used throughout OpenEMR to control access by user roles. It can be configured at Administration->ACL.

openemr_claim: Documents emergency (break glass) access permitting authorized users to reach records during an emergency; ONC (d)(6) Emergency Access certified per CHPL. Reason capture and automatic time-limited expiry are not detailed in primary docs.
quote: Permit authorized users (who are authorized for emergency situations) to access electronic health information during an emergency.

openemr_claim: no_documentation_found

Documents auditing of login/logout, session timeout, account lockout, patient record create/view/update/delete, query, order, signature, PHI export/import, security administration, and backup/restore events.
quote: User login/logout 3. Session timeout 4. Account lockout 5. Patient record created/viewed/updated/deleted 6. Scheduling 7. Query 8. Order 9. node-authentication failure 10. Signature created/validated 11. PHI export (e.g. print) 12. PHI import 13. Security administration events 14. Backup and restore

openemr_claim: Documents audit records protected from unauthorized deletion and modification, read-restricted to explicitly authorized users, logged to an IHE ATNA-profile audit engine; ONC (d)(3) Audit Report certified per CHPL.
quote: The system shall protect the stored audit records from unauthorized deletion. The system shall prevent modifications to the audit records.

openemr_claim: Documents integrity verification detecting alteration/deletion of health information and audit logs (SHA-1 or higher) plus per-record audit checksums; ONC (d)(8) Integrity certified per CHPL. Interface-payload integrity is not separately documented.
quote: Verify that electronic health information has not been altered in transit and detect the alteration and deletion of electronic health information and audit logs

openemr_claim: Documents encryption in transit (TLS/IPsec encrypted, integrity-protected link) and at rest (AES-128 or higher for backups/removable media); ONC (d)(7)/(d)(9)/(d)(12) certified per CHPL. Component-by-component coverage (queue, object store) is not enumerated.
quote: An encrypted and integrity protected link must be implemented (e.g., TLS, IPv6, IPv4 with IPsec).

quote: The tarball will contain the gzipped files openemr.sql.gz and openemr.tar.gz.

Documents a patient chart/portal presenting issues (problems, allergies, medications), demographics, history, and insurance; cross-site aggregation is within one shared database or via CCDA import, not a multi-instance longitudinal record.
quote: Issues (medical problems, allergies, medications etc.) are also supported.

openemr_claim: Documents CCDA import via the Carecoordination module with an Accept/Discard workflow into the patient record; item-level reconciliation (accept/reject/replace per item with provenance) is not documented, and OpenEMR 8 is NOT ONC-certified to (b)(2).
quote: 'Accept' or 'Discard' buttons (rectangle top center) takes the changes into the patient record or rejects the CCDA import.

openemr_claim: Documents e-signature that locks a form (and, optionally, an entire encounter) and disables editing, providing signed-note immutability; ONC (d)(4) Amendments certified per CHPL. Whether an amendment is an appended addendum vs a new linked record is discussed but not firmly specified.
quote: This will disable the edit button on any form that is e-signed. Default is ON

openemr_claim: Documents laboratory results with status values (preliminary, cannot be done, final, corrected, incomplete) and report statuses (received, complete, error) via HL7 v2.5.1 ORU; the full placed/accepted/collected order-state lifecycle with specimen identity is only partially documented.
quote: preliminary, cannot be done, final, corrected, incompete...etc.

openemr_claim: Documents medication ordering/prescribing (create and send prescriptions via e-prescribe/print/fax/email); ONC (a)(1) CPOE-Medications certified per CHPL. Field-level activation gating that blocks incomplete orders is not detailed in primary docs.
quote: Create and send prescriptions

openemr_claim: Documents drug-drug/drug-allergy interaction checking during CPOE, but implemented via the external eRx interface (NewCrop; RxNav/rxcui online lookups) rather than natively; OpenEMR 8 is NOT ONC-certified to (a)(4).
quote: Before a medication order is completed and acted upon during computerized provider order entry (CPOE), interventions must automatically and electronically indicate to a user drug-drug and drug-allergy contraindications based on a patient's medication list and medication allergy list.

openemr_claim: OpenEMR documents that it does NOT have an eMAR/MAR in the public version; barcoded medication-administration verification (BCMA) of patient/order/med/dose/route/time is not supported.
quote: OpenEMR does not have an eMAR as you would see in an in-patient setting such as a hospital.

openemr_claim: Documents CCDA-based medication import/merge via Carecoordination; structured admission/transfer/discharge medication reconciliation with continue/change/substitute/stop decisions and provenance is not documented, and OpenEMR 8 is NOT ONC-certified to (b)(2).
quote: When you receive a CCDA file you may create a new patient with it or merge the information in it with an existing patient's record.

openemr_claim: Documents e-prescribing via the WENO Exchange intermediary supporting NewRx and CancelRx (with EPCS as an add-on); RxRenewal and RxChange transactions are not documented, and OpenEMR 8 is NOT ONC-certified to (b)(3).
